meta_title: For Students: SaaS starter kit to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Starter Kits: Master App Building for Students
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between theory and production. Use Antigravity and Claude Code to build your first professional-grade SaaS starter kit with this guide.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the SaaS knowledge gap requires moving from abstract tutorials to production-ready starter kits. By leveraging Antigravity for architecture scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you can bypass weeks of boilerplate configuration, focusing instead on shipping your unique value proposition to users while mastering enterprise-grade deployment patterns.
content.intro:
You have spent months studying documentation, yet the leap to a production-grade SaaS feels like staring into an abyss of authentication flows, database migrations, and deployment pipelines. The gap between a hobby project and a professional application isn't just code; it's the architecture that separates a script from a scalable business.

Today, we stop building 'demos' and start building infrastructure. By utilizing an opinionated SaaS starter kit, you gain the skeletal structure of a professional app, allowing you to focus on the logic that actually matters. This isn't just about writing code; it's about adopting the workflow of a senior developer.

steps.title: Architecting the Core Stack
steps.description: Define your SaaS foundation using a proven starter kit template to ensure security and scalability are baked in from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-ready SaaS starter kit structure using Next.js 15, PostgreSQL (Prisma), and Clerk for authentication. Include a basic dashboard layout and user profile management.

steps.title: Implementing Role-Based Access
steps.description: Secure your application by defining access levels, preventing the common mistake of exposing administrative routes to standard users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a role-based access control (RBAC) middleware for the Next.js router. Create helper functions to check for 'admin' and 'premium' user roles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply rbac middleware to /app/admin routes'

steps.title: Building the Database Schema
steps.description: Model your data for long-term growth, ensuring efficient relational mapping between users, subscriptions, and core app entities.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Prisma schema that links Users to Subscriptions and Projects. Include cascading deletes and timestamps for all models.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema and push to local db'

steps.title: Integrating Payment Flows
steps.description: Handle the complexities of SaaS billing by integrating Stripe webhooks to manage subscription lifecycles automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up a Stripe webhook handler to update user subscription status in the database upon successful checkout completion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add stripe webhook handler for payment.succeeded event'

steps.title: Automating Production Deployments
steps.description: Configure your CI/CD pipeline so that every push to main automatically tests and deploys your code to a live environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs linting, tests, and deploys the application to Vercel upon merge to the main branch.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions deployment workflow'

pro_tips: Always use Environment Variables for API keys; never hardcode secrets, even in private repositories.
Implement 'Feature Flags' early so you can toggle experimental code in production without rolling back your entire deployment.
Focus on 'Database Normalization' early; un-doing a messy database schema after you have users is the most expensive mistake in SaaS.
Use 'Edge Functions' for lightweight API logic to reduce latency and minimize your server-side compute costs.
Adopt 'semantic versioning' for your API routes to ensure that your frontend doesn't break when you update your backend services.
content.next_steps: After completing this kit, deploy your app to a live URL, add a custom domain, and share your project repository on your portfolio to demonstrate your ability to manage production-ready infrastructure.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student; you are a builder. The industry rewards those who ship, not those who study. Take the leap, build your foundation, and watch your portfolio transform from theory into a tangible asset. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
